_doc: Qanat gallery + channel facts. Bare scalars drive geometry; _prov holds citations. Steps reference the shaft they sit under.

gradientPct.note: constant 0.1% (10 cm/100 m) from P9 to P-5, except two steps

channelStartFromP9M.note: no channel for the first ~13 m from the east

dropM.note: stepped fall / height provision under P6
gallery + channel drop 1 m below the limestone band under P4

researchStatus._doc: Visual reading of fig.6-4 (the 'status of the present research' schematic): which stretches of the main-line gallery have actually been explored/walked, vs. only inferred to exist between documented shafts. This is a DIFFERENT, finer-grained fact than the sections[] host/profile data above (from fig.5-15) — fig.6-4 shows the gallery alternating explored/unexplored TWICE (not once): grey P-5→P-1, black P-1→P2, grey P2→P4, black P4→P9.

_prov.note: Digitised from the figure's black (explored) / grey (unexplored) bar coding; segment boundaries read at the shaft they visually break on (one break, between P-4 and P-1, falls between shafts, not on one), not measured to the metre.

_provNoMarker.note: These four shafts carry no construction-work square in the figure at all (unlike every other main-line shaft, which all show a filled marker) — read as 'not applicable' rather than 'not done'.

constructionWorkExceptions.note: Drawn hollow in the figure — and, uniquely, the whole P-5 shaft body is drawn in the construction-work red rather than black/grey — sitting directly beside the 'channel enclosure and visitor's gallery' (b) annotation, itself also drawn hollow. Reads as planned/not-yet-built, unlike every other shaft's filled marker.

shaftExplored._doc: Each SHAFT's own body is ALSO drawn black/grey in the figure — a separate, per-shaft fact from exploredSegments above (which is about the connecting GALLERY reach, not the shaft itself). The two don't always agree at the same shaft (e.g. P-4's shaft is black even though the gallery segment it sits in is grey) — read as 'has this shaft itself been explored/opened', distinct from 'has the tunnel reaching it been walked'. P-5 is omitted here — its whole body is drawn in the construction-work red instead of black/grey (see shaftSpecial).

shaftCollarDepthM._doc: P-1 and P4 are each drawn with a short BLACK 'explored' segment starting at the surface, above an otherwise-grey/unexplored body (shaftExplored above gives their overall body colour) — a finer-grained fact than a single per-shaft colour, and a real excavated depth, not a cosmetic cap. depthM is INFERRED, not measured: read each shaft's black-segment and total-bar pixel heights directly off fig.6-4, then calibrated a px/metre scale from the three main-line shafts in this SAME figure with an independently documented real depth (P1=30 m, P3=35 m, P5=36 m → 10.0-11.1 px/m, ~10% internal spread), then applied that scale to P-1/P4's measured pixels. A shallower calibration shaft (P-4, 12 m documented) gave a very different scale (15.4 px/m) — fig.6-4 is explicitly schematic/not-to-scale, so this degrades further outside the 30-36 m range the calibration set covers. No other shaft in the figure shows this split coding.

P-1.note: Implied total shaft depth from the same calibration (~19 m) falls in an uncalibrated gap between the shallow auxiliary shafts (6-8 m) and the deep main shafts (30-36 m) — order-of-magnitude estimate only.

P4.note: Implied total shaft depth from the same calibration (~35 m) falls within the calibrated 30-36 m range (P1/P3/P5), so the proportion this depth is read from is on firmer ground than P-1's.

overflowChannel.relationToMain: branches off the main channel at the P-4 overflow weir; when the main channel dams up above ~12 cm, the surplus spills over the weir into this lateral (deviation) channel

surfaceOutlet.name: surface spring (overflow resurgence)
surfaceOutlet.note: The ~80 m³/d diverted at the P-4 weir stays a subterranean lateral drain near the channel level (~355 m) and resurfaces DOWNSLOPE of the weir. Field-surveyed outlet: 6.147987 E / 49.666272 N (WGS84); LUREF 78546 E / 81300 N; UTM32N 294207 / 5505432. Supersedes the earlier illustrative daylight point.

gpsStatus.note: field-surveyed GPS of the resurgence outlet (WGS84 / LUREF / UTM32N provided)

diameterCm.note: Ø 80 cm lateral channel at the P-4 weir

triggerLevelCm.note: weir diverts when the level exceeds ~12 cm

divertedFlowM3PerDay.note: ~80 m³/d deviated via the P-4 weir (hydrology.deviationAtP4M3PerDay)

documentedDistances._doc: The only metric horizontal distances the brochure states in text (used to sanity-check the schematic modelPos spacing; see pd-schematic-positions). The brochure gives NO shaft coordinates, so these plus fig.3-1's 100 m scale bar are the whole positional record.

distanceM.note: P3 exactly on the P1–P5 line, 28 m from P2, depth 35 m
P-7A located about 25 m northwest of P-5; modelPos re-spaced to honour this (~3.6 units at 7 m/unit)

items.note: Haedchen marshy source area ~375 masl, 600 m east of the Pëtschend spur (~395 masl).
